  f(x) = k xᵖ  

where k and p are constants.

1.  f(x) = 2x · 4x³  
  2x · 4x³ = 8x⁴ → of the form k xᵖ (k = 8, p = 4).  
  Hence f(x) is a power function.

2.  g(x) = –x⁵ + 5x³  
  This is the sum of two different power terms; it cannot be written as a single k xᵖ.  
  Hence g(x) is not a power function.

3.  h(x) = (2x⁵ – 1)/(3x² + 4)  
  This rational expression cannot be reduced to one term k xᵖ.  
  Hence h(x) is not a power function.

Therefore, among the three, only f(x) is a power function.
